description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with two stages: validate and build. In the validate stage add an inspec-tests job using the chef/inspec:2.2.27 image (entrypoint /bin/sh -c) that runs "cd infrastructure/inspec-profiles" then iterates over each subdirectory with "for p in $(ls -d */); do echo \"Checking ${p}\" && inspec check ${p}; done". Also add a packer-templates job using the hashicorp/packer:1.2.4 image (entrypoint /bin/sh -c) that runs "cd infrastructure/images" followed by "for t in $(ls | grep .json$); do echo \"Validating ${t}\" && packer validate ${t}; done". In the build stage add a build:jenkins-master job that uses the docker:18.03 image with the docker:dind service, logs in to registry.gitlab.com with "docker login -u gitlab-ci-token -p $CI_JOB_TOKEN registry.gitlab.com", pulls the latest image if present, builds a new image from docker/jenkins-master/ with "--cache-from $CONTAINER_IMAGE:latest" and tags it as $CONTAINER_IMAGE:$CI_BUILD_REF and $CONTAINER_IMAGE:latest, then pushes both tags; it runs only on the master branch and defines variables CONTAINER_IMAGE=registry.gitlab.com/merlodicus/jenkins-docker/master, DOCKER_DRIVE=overlay2, DOCKER_HOST=tcp://docker:2375. Also add a build:jenkins-nginx job identical to the previous one but using CONTAINER_IMAGE=registry.gitlab.com/merlodicus/jenkins-docker/nginx and building from docker/jenkins-nginx/, also limited to the master branch.
